name: Special Interest Groups | openEuler
description: Discover openEuler's SIGs covering Linux, cloud, AI, and more. Collaborate on diverse projects with SIG members.

name: openEuler SIG Group Directory
description: Discover openEuler's SIGs covering Linux, cloud, AI, and more. Collaborate on diverse projects with SIG members.

acceptedAnswer.text: openEuler SIGs (Special Interest Groups) are the fundamental collaborative units of the openEuler open-source community, each focusing on code maintenance, review, and release management in specific technical domains.

mainEntity.name: How to join an openEuler SIG?

acceptedAnswer.text: Join an existing SIG by submitting a PR to its repository. To create a new SIG, submit a PR to the openEuler/community repository; after TC review, approval typically takes about 2 weeks.

mainEntity.name: How many SIG groups does openEuler have?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of 2025, the openEuler community has over 100 SIG groups covering core technical areas such as kernel, virtualization, cloud native, embedded, AI, and security.

mainEntity.name: What roles exist in openEuler SIG groups?

acceptedAnswer.text: Main SIG roles include: Maintainer (responsible for final code merges), Committer (has merge permissions for specific scopes), and Contributor (participates through Pull Requests).
